questions.question_text: A Colorado trust account must be what type of account?
questions.explanation: Trust accounts must be demand (checking) accounts at a Colorado financial institution. Savings, money market, and investment accounts are not permitted.

questions.question_text: A broker deposits a personal check into the brokerage trust account to cover a temporary shortfall. This is:
questions.explanation: Commingling personal funds with trust funds is prohibited in Colorado. Even temporary deposits of personal funds violate trust account rules.

questions.question_text: The trust account label must include the broker's name and which words?
questions.explanation: The trust account must display the broker's name and the words 'trust account.' This clearly identifies the account as holding client funds separate from the broker's personal funds.

questions.question_text: Which brokers in Colorado must maintain a trust account?
questions.correct_answer: Every employing broker handling others' money
distractors: All licensed brokers regardless of duties
questions.explanation: Every employing broker who handles money belonging to others must maintain a trust account. If a broker never handles client funds, no trust account is required.

questions.question_text: A Colorado trust account must be maintained at:
distractors: A bank within the broker's county
questions.explanation: Trust accounts must be held at a Colorado financial institution. An out-of-state bank, even if FDIC-insured, would not satisfy the requirement.

questions.question_text: If a contract does not specify when earnest money must be delivered, it must be delivered within:
questions.correct_answer: A reasonable time, typically 3 business days
questions.explanation: When the contract does not specify, earnest money must be delivered within a reasonable time, which is typically interpreted as within 3 business days of contract acceptance.

questions.question_text: Earnest money in Colorado is typically delivered to:
questions.correct_answer: The broker or a title company
questions.explanation: Earnest money deposits are delivered to the listing broker's trust account or to a title company, as specified in the contract.

questions.question_text: A buyer signs a contract on Monday specifying delivery of earnest money within 5 business days. The earnest money is due by:
questions.explanation: Five business days from Monday would be the following Monday (Tue, Wed, Thu, Fri, Mon). The contract timeline controls when specified.

questions.question_text: A broker receives earnest money but delays depositing it for two weeks without cause. This delay:
questions.correct_answer: May violate trust account delivery rules
distractors: Is permitted if the check does not bounce
Only matters if a complaint is filed
questions.explanation: Earnest money must be delivered per contract terms or within a reasonable time. Unexplained two-week delays violate the delivery requirement and could trigger discipline.

questions.question_text: The contract specifies that earnest money will be held by the title company. The listing broker should:
questions.correct_answer: Ensure delivery to the title company
distractors: Deposit it in their own trust account
Return it to the buyer for safekeeping
questions.explanation: The broker must follow the contract terms. If the contract specifies the title company as the custodian, the earnest money should be delivered there, not the broker's trust account.

questions.question_text: How long must a Colorado broker retain trust account records after closing?
questions.explanation: Trust account and transaction records must be maintained for 4 years from the date of closing, or from the date of last activity if no closing occurred.

questions.question_text: Trust account reconciliations in Colorado must be performed:
questions.explanation: Brokers must perform monthly reconciliations of trust account records, including receipts, disbursements, and running balances.

questions.question_text: The Commission announces it will audit a broker's trust account. Can the broker refuse?
questions.correct_answer: No — Commission can audit at any time
distractors: Yes, unless a complaint was filed
Yes, if the broker hires an attorney
questions.explanation: The Commission has the authority to audit trust accounts at any time. Refusing an audit could result in additional disciplinary action.

questions.question_text: A transaction falls through and never closes. The broker must retain the records for:
questions.explanation: Records must be maintained for 4 years from closing, or from the date of last activity if no closing occurs. Failed transactions still require record retention.

questions.question_text: Which records must a Colorado broker maintain for each transaction?
distractors: Only trust account deposits and withdrawals
questions.explanation: Complete transaction records include listing agreements, contracts, correspondence, disclosures, and closing documents — not just a single document.
